summary: Italian Renaissance painter, court artist to the Gonzaga family in Mantua. Known for the Dead Christ (extreme foreshortening), Camera degli Sposi (di sotto in su ceiling), Triumphs of Caesar, St. Sebastian (three versions), and San Zeno Altarpiece.

works.name: Lamentation over the Dead Christ
works.description: Extremely foreshortened body of Christ on a marble slab with prominent nail wounds on feet. Mary, St. John, and Mary Magdalene weep at the left. In Milan's Brera Gallery. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Extremely foreshortened body of Christ on marble slab; prominent nail wounds on hands and feet; two/three mourners at left (Mary, John, Magdalene)
examples: the central figure's hands and feet are propped up to show his wounds while resting on a marble slab
Mary, Saint John, and Mary Magdalene weeping over an extremely foreshortened Jesus

clues.clue: Man Lying on a Slab is a preparatory study drawing
examples: In a study for a major work this artist drew a man resting on his left elbow called Man Lying on a Slab

clues.clue: Housed in Milan's Brera Gallery
examples: housed in Milan's Brera Gallery

works.name: Camera degli Sposi (c. 1465-1474)
works.description: Frescoed room in the Ducal Palace of Mantua for patron Ludovico Gonzaga. Ceiling uses di sotto in su technique to depict an oculus opening to a blue sky with putti. Court Scene depicts Gonzaga family. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Di sotto in su ceiling with oculus opening to blue sky, surrounded by putti; peacock, turbaned man looking down
examples: uses the di sotto in su technique to depict an oculus opening up to a cloudy sky
A peacock and a man in a turban are depicted next to several foreshortened cherubs

clues.clue: Court Scene: patron Ludovico Gonzaga turning in chair to speak to secretary; family portrait including dwarf
examples: This artist depicted his patron turning in his chair to speak to his secretary in a family portrait called The Court Scene

clues.clue: Portraits of first eight Roman emperors as grisaille roundels; artist's self-portrait among floral vines
examples: marble putti holding up wreathed portraits of the first eight Roman emperors

clues.clue: Portraits of Frederick III and Christian I of Denmark added at last minute
examples: At the last minute, this artist added portraits of Frederick III and Christian I of Denmark

clues.clue: In the Palazzo Ducale in Mantua; originally called Camera Picta (not actually a bridal chamber)
examples: no longer thought to have been a bridal chamber, despite its name

works.name: Triumphs of Caesar (c. 1484-1492)
works.description: Nine-panel series depicting Caesar's victory procession. Now at Hampton Court Palace. Includes SPQR banners, slaves, vases, spoils. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Nine-panel series depicting Caesar's victory procession; now at Hampton Court Palace; includes SPQR banners, slaves, vases, spoils, man on elephant
examples: Trumpets displaying SPQR banners fly above men carrying loot in the fourth entry in a nine-part series
nine paintings now housed at Hampton Court which depict people parading slaves, vases, and other spoils from Caesar's victory in Gaul

clues.clue: Commissioned by Gonzaga family for their palace
examples: commissioned for the palace of this artist's patron

clues.clue: Andrea Andreani produced a 12-foot-wide chiaroscuro print of the series
examples: Andrea Andreani produced a 12-foot-wide print of this artist's nine-panel series

clues.clue: 2020 preparatory sketch discovered showing two bugle players with SPQR banners
examples: A preparatory sketch by this artist discovered in 2020 shows two bugle players holding SPQR banners

clues.clue: Imitated colored marble backdrops with reddish splotched canvas; includes depiction of Cybele's arrival in Rome
examples: This artist imitated colored marble backdrops with a reddish splotched canvas in works like a depiction of Cybele's arrival in Rome

works.name: St. Sebastian (three versions)
works.description: Three paintings of St. Sebastian tied to a column/arch, riddled with arrows. Located in Vienna, Venice, and the Louvre. One version has a rider/death figure in the clouds. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Three versions of St. Sebastian tied to a column, pierced by arrows; located in Vienna, Venice, and the Louvre
examples: He painted three versions of the martyrdom of a certain saint, which are located in Vienna, Venice, and the Louvre
This artist uniquely used an arch as the backdrop for one of his three paintings

clues.clue: One version has a rider/scythe-carrying man (Death) in the clouds; anachronistic Roman ruins behind
examples: A scythe-carrying man on a horse can be seen in the clouds to the left of the pillar
Anachronistic Roman ruins are visible behind a contrapposto figure tied to a column

clues.clue: Metallic-shaded clothing in depictions
examples: This artist painted metallic-shaded clothing on three depictions of St. Sebastian

works.name: San Zeno Altarpiece (1456-1459)
works.description: Triptych for the Basilica of San Zeno in Verona. Four Corinthian columns divide three scenes connected by hanging garlands. Central predella depicts the Crucifixion. Pseudo-Kufic script on Mary's halo. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Four Corinthian columns divide three scenes connected by hanging garlands; sacra conversazione; for Basilica of San Zeno in Verona
examples: Four Corinthian columns divide three scenes connected by hanging garlands in this artist's Saint Zeno Altarpiece
An altarpiece by this artist shows the crucifixion in the central predella and a sacra conversazione

clues.clue: Mary's halo and robe hems contain pseudo-Kufic script; Oriental rug beneath her
examples: both Mary's halo and the hems of her robe contain pseudo-Kufic script, and an Oriental rug lies beneath her

clues.clue: Predellas stolen by the French and replaced with copies
examples: The predellas of an altarpiece by this artist were replaced with copies after they were stolen by the French

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Court artist to the Gonzaga family in Mantua. Brother-in-law of Giovanni Bellini. Other works: Parnassus (Mars and Venus on arch; for Isabella d'Este's studiolo), Triumph of the Virtues / Minerva Driving Away Vice, Judith and Holofernes, St. James Led to His Execution (destroyed 1944, Ovetari Chapel), Agony in the Garden, Adoration of the Magi, Battle of the Sea Gods.

clues.clue: Court artist to the Gonzaga family in Mantua
examples: This artist for the Gonzagas

clues.clue: Brother-in-law of Giovanni Bellini
examples: These two fifteenth century artists who were brothers-in-law

clues.clue: Parnassus: Mars and Venus on a natural arch; for Isabella d'Este's studiolo; Anteros shoots blow dart at Vulcan's genitals; Muses dance while Apollo plays lyre
examples: Mars and Venus stand atop a natural arch in a Mantegna painting made for Isabella's Studiolo

clues.clue: Triumph of the Virtues / Minerva Driving Away Vice from Garden of Virtue: Diana carried off by centaur as Minerva chases vices; African, centaur, hermaphrodite monkey
examples: An African and a centaur are among the figures fleeing from the title location

clues.clue: St. James Led to His Execution: destroyed in 1944 WWII bombing of Ovetari Chapel in Padua; extremely low worm's-eye viewpoint
examples: his now-destroyed St. James Led to His Execution
St. James Led to His Execution has an extremely low viewpoint, and was destroyed in 1944

clues.clue: Engraver: Battle of the Sea Gods, Virgin in the Grotto
examples: He was also known as an engraver, creating such works as a depiction of a bacchanal and the Battle of the Sea Gods

clues.clue: Presentation of Christ in the Temple: infant in mummy-like white cloth; Bellini traced Mantegna's version
examples: Mantegna painted a version of this scene showing an infant swaddled in mummy-looking white cloth

clues.clue: Adoration of the Magi: Balthazar wears leopard-skin turban
examples: exoticized Africans with works like his Adoration of the Magi, in which Balthazar wears a leopard-skin turban

comprehensive_summary:
Andrea Mantegna was an Italian Renaissance painter, court artist to the Gonzaga family in Mantua, and brother-in-law of Giovanni Bellini. He is most commonly identified by The Dead Christ (extreme foreshortening) and the Camera degli Sposi.

The Lamentation over the Dead Christ shows an extremely foreshortened body on a marble slab with prominent nail wounds on the feet, with Mary, St. John, and Mary Magdalene weeping at the left. It is housed in Milan's Brera Gallery.

The Camera degli Sposi is a frescoed room in the Ducal Palace of Mantua. Its ceiling uses the di sotto in su technique depicting an oculus opening to a blue sky surrounded by putti, a peacock, and a turbaned man. The Court Scene shows patron Ludovico Gonzaga and his family, including a dwarf.

The Triumphs of Caesar is a nine-panel series depicting Caesar's victory procession with SPQR banners, slaves, and spoils, now at Hampton Court Palace. His three versions of St. Sebastian (Vienna, Venice, Louvre) show the saint tied to a column pierced by arrows, one with a rider/Death figure in clouds.

The San Zeno Altarpiece is a triptych with four Corinthian columns dividing scenes connected by garlands, with the Crucifixion as the central predella and pseudo-Kufic script on Mary's halo.

Other important works include Parnassus (Mars and Venus on a natural arch, for Isabella d'Este's studiolo), Triumph of the Virtues, St. James Led to His Execution (destroyed in 1944 bombing of Ovetari Chapel), engravings like Battle of the Sea Gods, Adoration of the Magi, Judith and Holofernes, and Agony in the Garden.
